subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Help the Alphabet Forest Ranger match letter cards to the sounds animals hear at the reading trail.
lesson.objective: I can identify uppercase and lowercase letters and match common letters to their sounds.
lesson.concept_explanation: Letters are symbols. Each letter has a name, an uppercase form, a lowercase form, and one or more sounds. In this mission, we use common beginning sounds to connect print to speech.
lesson.common_misconception: A letter name is not always the same as the sound it makes, and uppercase and lowercase partners can look different.
lesson.real_world_connection: We use letters and beginning sounds when reading signs, names, labels, and books.
lesson.lesson_summary: Look at the letter shape, name the letter, then say or choose its common sound.
lesson.mini_lesson: First look at the letter card. Decide if it is uppercase or lowercase. Then connect the printed letter to a common sound, like M says /m/ in moon.

audio.text: Listen, then say it with me.
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Watch: The card shows M / m. M is the uppercase letter. Moon begins with /m/, so M matches /m/.
Demo: The picture is sun. Sun begins with /s/. The letter S makes /s/, so choose S.

hint_ladder: Look at the letter shape first.
Say the letter name, then say the common sound.
Use the picture clue to check the beginning sound.

hints: Look closely at the letter or picture.
Say the letter name and the sound slowly.
Check that the sound and symbol match.
guided_practice.explanation: Uppercase M is the capital form of the letter.

question_audio.text: Which card shows uppercase letter M?
question_audio.label: Read Question

hints: Look closely at the letter or picture.
Say the letter name and the sound slowly.
Check that the sound and symbol match.
guided_practice.explanation: Lowercase t is the small form that matches uppercase T.

question_audio.text: Which card shows lowercase letter T?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: What common sound does S make?
hints: Look closely at the letter or picture.
Say the letter name and the sound slowly.
Check that the sound and symbol match.
guided_practice.explanation: S commonly makes the /s/ sound as in sun.

question_audio.text: Which letter makes the sss sound?
question_audio.label: Read Question

hints: Look closely at the letter or picture.

misconception_bank.letter_name_confusion: Learners may say a letter name when the question asks for a sound.
misconception_bank.uppercase_lowercase_confusion: Learners may not connect uppercase and lowercase forms of the same letter.
misconception_bank.sound_symbol_confusion: Learners may know a sound but choose a different printed letter.
misconception_bank.visually_similar_letter_confusion: Learners may mix letters that look alike, such as b and d or p and q.

letter_name_confusion: Ask: Is the question asking for the letter name or the sound?
uppercase_lowercase_confusion: Pair the capital and small form on one card.
sound_symbol_confusion: Say the sound, then trace the letter that makes it.
visually_similar_letter_confusion: Compare the tall stick, circle, and direction of each letter.

game_theme.title: Alphabet Forest Sound Scout
game_theme.name: Alphabet Forest Sound Scout
game_theme.mission_intro: Find letter cards, listen for beginning sounds, and earn the Sound Scout Badge.
